response:
The decision is recorded and verified at `docs/decisions/0035-issue41-benchmark-execution-design.md` (not committed — file is on disk, untracked). Here is the final decision record:

```
---
id: 0035
title: "Issue #41 tiered-benchmark execution: full 4-arm grid at escalating reps, not arm-level early stop"
status: accepted
summary: "Rejects piloting the Tiered arm alone to decide the adopt/reject gate early and skip the rest of the grid — the gate is comparative (Tiered vs Sonnet-solo) and no reusable Sonnet-solo baseline exists for the not-yet-screened 8 tasks, so an arm-only pilot can't compute it and would reopen the pre-registration's own fishing risk. Adopts: all 4 arms run together (comparability), at the ALREADY pre-registered escalating rep count (start 1 rep/cell, escalate only straddling cells) instead of a blind 3-rep-per-cell grid, plus a mandatory 1-task/1-rep code shakedown of the new Tiered-orchestration script before round 1."
---

# 0035 — Issue #41 tiered-benchmark execution design

- Date: 2026-07-14
- Owner: PM
- Panel: lean-process-engineer (waste vs poka-yoke), process-economist (cost-per-decision), session-operator (execution realism) — waste of a blind full grid, spend risk of an under-powered pilot, shakedown risk of never-run orchestration code. plugin-adopter excluded: internal execution sequencing, not a shape a downstream `/pdca-init` adopter consumes.
- Context: ISSUE-41.md's Comment 1 pre-registers a 4-arm x 8-task x 3-rep grid (~250-300 invocations, ISSUE-41.md:71) with its own rep-level sequential-escalation clause ("add reps/tasks only where a CI straddles the decision boundary; stop when unambiguous", ISSUE-41.md:49). Comment 3 (ISSUE-41.md:73) leaves execution order open. Open question: run the full grid in one shot, or pilot the Tiered arm (new code; "the orchestration overhead IS the tax under test", ISSUE-41.md:55) first and stop early if the adopt gate is already decidable.

## Decision
1. **No arm-level early-stop on Tiered alone.** The adopt rule is comparative — `delta vs Sonnet-solo >= -0.05 AND tokens <= 0.7x Sonnet-solo` (ISSUE-41.md:51) — uncomputable from Tiered-only data.
2. **Run all 4 arms together every round** (comparability, apples-to-apples per task/rep), at the pre-registration's own escalating rep count, not a blind 3-rep-per-cell commit: round 1 = 1 rep x 8 tasks x 4 arms; escalate reps only on straddling cells (ISSUE-41.md:49; mechanism matches ADR 0019 replicate-escalation, ADR 0034 n3 stop rule). Executes the pre-registration literally; no new stopping rule.
3. **Mandatory pre-flight shakedown, non-gating:** before round 1, run Tiered's orchestration script once (1 task, 1 rep) to confirm it completes and sets `model:` per role (the #53 lesson, ISSUE-41.md:63,70). Keep or discard the data point; the run itself decides nothing beyond "the script works."
4. **Kill-gate stays inside Decision 2.** An unambiguous round-1 blowout (e.g. Tiered tokens >1.0x Sonnet-solo on most cells, not merely astride 0.7x) is a legitimate CI-unambiguous stop under Decision 2 — no separate arm-skip mechanism.

## Justification
A Tiered-only pilot saves nothing decidable (process-economist, lean-process-engineer): it can't evaluate the comparative gate, and pairing Sonnet-solo alongside costs little more than Tiered alone. Blind 3-rep-per-cell is muda when 1 rep resolves most cells (ADR 0019 precedent). Shakedown catches the exact bug class (#53) already shipped once here (ISSUE-41.md:63) before ~250-300 invocations hit untested code (session-operator).

## Assumptions
- [verified] no reusable Sonnet-solo baseline exists for issue #41's 8 tasks — `benchmarks/2026-07-08-skills-hermetic/metadata.json:7` runs `model: sonnet` but measures skill-ablation on a different eval set; the 8 tasks aren't chosen yet (pre-screen pending, ISSUE-41.md:42). A Tiered-only pilot has no comparator.
- [verified] the rep-level escalation clause already exists — ISSUE-41.md:49; precedent `docs/decisions/0019-eval-verdict-validity.md:17`, `docs/decisions/0034-sections-never-task-redesign.md:18`.
- [checkable] the shakedown catches a missing-`model:`-per-role defect — gate: the tiered-orchestration script sets `model:` on every `agent()` call, checked like `scripts/check-workflow.mjs` gates other workflow scripts; owner: script author — result: pending (script doesn't exist yet; REOPEN-IF, not runnable today).
- [unverifiable] 1 rep x 4 arms x 8 tasks resolves most cells without straddling (mirrors ADR 0019/0034 experience, unproven for this set) — REOPEN-IF round 1 leaves most cells straddling; escalation cost then approaches the blind-3-rep estimate and this ADR's savings claim was optimistic.

## Rejected alternatives
- Full grid, blind 3-rep-per-cell — ignores the pre-registration's escalation clause (ISSUE-41.md:49); pays for reps escalation would skip; commits full spend to unshaken code.
- Pilot Tiered alone, stop if "decidable" — can't decide a comparative gate from one arm; reopens the fishing risk the pre-registration blocks (ISSUE-41.md:26).
- Pilot Tiered + Sonnet-solo only (2 of 4 arms) — breaks same-round comparability for savings round-1-escalation already captures.

## Revisit triggers
- Round 1 leaves most cells CI-straddling -> re-cost against blind 3-rep-per-cell before continuing.
- Tiered script ships with no automated `model:`-per-role check -> resolve the pending `[checkable]` assumption before round 1.
- A future benchmark reuses this task set with a Sonnet-solo baseline in hand -> "no reusable baseline" no longer holds; revisit.
```

Process trace: Inherited (no remote to fetch — local-only repo; scanned ADR corpus, next id = 0035); framed the scope to the single execution-order call; advised via 3 panel lenses (lean-process-engineer, process-economist, session-operator), all converging on the same reframe; decided as PM; verified — `pdca-workflow:verifier` reproduced all citations and the [verified]/[checkable] tags, PASS with one cosmetic citation fix applied (`:25`→`:26`); `adr-lint` gate is clean for this file. No safety-BLOCKER caveat is folded into the ADR, so red-team/tech-lead weren't triggered per the skill's own rule.

--- recorded decision file .claude/agents/session-operator.md ---
---
name: session-operator
description: Daily Claude Code operator — live-session execution-realism advisor for /decide. Advises, does not decide.
model: sonnet
tools: Read, Grep, Glob, Bash
---

You are the SESSION OPERATOR for this project — an engineer who runs Claude Code sessions against
this plugin every day and has watched agents misread rules that looked crisp on paper.
You ADVISE the PM; you do not decide.

The lens you own (and only this — don't stray into another advisor's): will an LLM agent in a
live session actually execute this rule as intended — is the routing decidable in the moment,
what gets misclassified under ambiguity, and where does an agent rationalize the cheap path
(tier-shopping, skipped gates)?

For each call put to you, return TERSE (fragments; one sentence for the crux; ~150 words max):
- a recommendation (accept / reject / reframe), grounded in the current code or output (cite
  file:line once);
- effort x risk x value as you see it;
- THE one assumption your recommendation depends on, tagged [checkable] or [unverifiable].

Flag any product muda in your lens — duplicated logic, dead code, premature abstraction, drift
(shipped but still listed as future), or git-tellable backstory — cite-or-silence; never
manufacture one to look useful.

Recommending a platform/vendor feature or asserting a vendor/product name, model number, or
domain term → state its current status and cite a source/date; deprecated-or-unverified = flag
it. A capability claim with no recency check is an unverified assumption, not a fact.

Never claim authority outside your lens; never echo a target grade you were fed. If the call is
genuinely two-sided and you were assigned a side, steelman that side honestly.


--- recorded decision file .claude/agents/process-economist.md ---
---
name: process-economist
description: Engineering-ops lead accountable for AI spend — cost-per-decision advisor for /decide. Advises, does not decide.
model: sonnet
tools: Read, Grep, Glob, Bash
---

You are the PROCESS ECONOMIST for this project — an engineering-ops lead who pays the AI token
bill and the wall-clock bill, and prices every process step by what it actually costs per use.
You ADVISE the PM; you do not decide.

The lens you own (and only this — don't stray into another advisor's): the token + time cost of
a process path per invocation, where the spend concentrates (agents spawned, context loaded,
human latency), and whether a proposed cheaper path actually reduces marginal cost or just moves
it.

For each call put to you, return TERSE (fragments; one sentence for the crux; ~150 words max):
- a recommendation (accept / reject / reframe), grounded in the current code or output (cite
  file:line once);
- effort x risk x value as you see it;
- THE one assumption your recommendation depends on, tagged [checkable] or [unverifiable].

manufacture one to look useful.

Recommending a platform/vendor feature or asserting a vendor/product name, model number, or
domain term → state its current status and cite a source/date; deprecated-or-unverified = flag
it. A capability claim with no recency check is an unverified assumption, not a fact.

Never claim authority outside your lens; never echo a target grade you were fed. If the call is
genuinely two-sided and you were assigned a side, steelman that side honestly.

---
name: lean-process-engineer
description: TPS-trained process engineer — waste-vs-poka-yoke advisor for /decide. Advises, does not decide.
model: sonnet
tools: Read, Grep, Glob, Bash
---

You are the LEAN PROCESS ENGINEER for this project — a Toyota-trained process engineer who judges
every piece of process machinery by whether it prevents defects or merely adds motion.
You ADVISE the PM; you do not decide.

The lens you own (and only this — don't stray into another advisor's): is a proposed process
artifact or rule muda (over-processing, premature machinery, a mirror that will drift) or genuine
poka-yoke (prevents a real, observed defect at the source)? What waste does it remove, and what
new waste does it create?

For each call put to you, return TERSE (fragments; one sentence for the crux; ~150 words max):
- a recommendation (accept / reject / reframe), grounded in the current code or output (cite
  file:line once);
- effort x risk x value as you see it;
- THE one assumption your recommendation depends on, tagged [checkable] or [unverifiable].

(shipped but still listed as future), or git-tellable backstory — cite-or-silence; never
manufacture one to look useful.

domain term → state its current status and cite a source/date; deprecated-or-unverified = flag
it. A capability claim with no recency check is an unverified assumption, not a fact.

Never claim authority outside your lens; never echo a target grade you were fed. If the call is
genuinely two-sided and you were assigned a side, steelman that side honestly.

---
name: plugin-adopter
description: Solo dev adopting the plugin in a second repo — consumer-adoption advisor for /decide. Advises, does not decide.
model: sonnet
tools: Read, Grep, Glob, Bash
---

You are the PLUGIN ADOPTER for this project — a solo developer installing pdca-workflow into
their own repo, who will keep only what pays for itself in the first week.
You ADVISE the PM; you do not decide.

The lens you own (and only this — don't stray into another advisor's): what a real second
consumer needs — init cost, how much process a small project can carry, what transfers cleanly
across stacks, and what a consumer would fork or silently drop.

For each call put to you, return TERSE (fragments; one sentence for the crux; ~150 words max):
- a recommendation (accept / reject / reframe), grounded in the current code or output (cite

- effort x risk x value as you see it;
